item.title: "Worst case scenario" for shippers as U.S. and Iran step up attacks
item.description: As the U.S. and Iran escalate attacks 140 days into the war, an industry analyst says "nobody is willing to move" through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Boomer wealth transfer will mostly benefit the already rich, report finds
item.description: Trillions of wealth passed down by the baby boomers will likely go to younger Americans who are already rich. Here's how much they'll get.

item.title: Maps show wildfire smoke forecast, air quality alerts in swath of U.S.
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and the U.S. East Coast this week.

item.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak traced to lettuce from Mexico used by Taco Bell
item.description: A lettuce supplier to fast-food giant Taco Bell has been linked to a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak that has sickened thousands of people, the CDC said.

item.title: Protests in Ukraine after Zelenskyy fires popular defense minister
item.description: Crowds took to Ukraine's streets to protest Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's dismissal of popular defense minister Mykhailo Fedorov. CBS News' Aidan Stretch reports.

item.title: Oil markets fluctuate as tensions between U.S. and Iran flare
item.description: Gas prices have increased again as tensions rise between the U.S. and Iran. Axios reporter Madison Mills explains how oil markets have reacted to the war.

item.title: Diarrhea-causing parasite outbreak possibly tied to iceberg lettuce from Mexico
item.description: FDA investigators believe iceberg lettuce from Mexico may be behind the spread of cyclosporiasis, a diarrhea-causing parasite. CBS News' Ash-har Quraishi reports.

item.title: Where peace talks stand as U.S., Iran continue days of strikes
item.description: The U.S. and Iran continued to exchange strikes as negotiations for a potential peace deal continued. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata has more.

item.title: ICE agent who fatally shot Maine man has history of violent behavior, AP reports
item.description: The ICE officer who shot and killed a Maine man has a history of violent behavior, the Associated Press reports, citing interviews with family and court records. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the latest.

item.title: Brenda Fricker, Oscar winner who played Pigeon Lady in "Home Alone 2," dies
item.description: Brenda Fricker won an Academy Award for 1989's "My Left Foot," played the Pigeon Lady in "Home Alone 2" and appeared in "A Time to Kill" and "So I Married an Axe Murderer."

item.title: Did China interfere in the 2020 election? Here's a fact check of Trump's speech
item.description: President Trump alleged that China interfered in the 2020 election during his primetime speech. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more on Mr. Trump's claims and foreign correspondent Anna Coren reports on Beijing's reaction.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 19)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Can you spot an AI image? Quiz shows how difficult identifying deepfakes has become.
item.description: An AI expert said the telltale signs people once relied on, such as distorted fingers, unnatural eyes, or other obvious visual flaws, have largely disappeared as technology has improved.

item.title: Coca-Cola temporarily halts production of Fairlife milk after cyberattack
item.description: A cybersecurity incident has forced Coca-Cola to suspend Fairlife milk production in the U.S. An investigation is underway.

item.title: Can creditors change their minds after agreeing to forgive a debt?
item.description: Before you assume a forgiven debt is settled, there are certain things to understand about the process.

item.title: Airlines say permanent daylight saving time won't be simple
item.description: Adjusting to permanent daylight saving time would cause significant disruptions to schedules and operations, an airline trade group said.

item.title: Anne Hathaway and Matt Damon give inside look at "The Odyssey" and challenges during filming
item.description: Anne Hathaway and Matt Damon, who star in "The Odyssey," speak to "CBS Mornings" about the movie, the challenges they faced during filming and working with Oscar-winning director Christopher Nolan.

item.title: Appeals court keeps in place Pentagon's escort policy for reporters
item.description: The appeals court in Washington, D.C., divided 2-1 in ruling in favor of the Trump administration in the legal battle over the Pentagon's escort policy for journalists.

item.title: When is hiring a debt relief company worth the cost?
item.description: Pursuing debt relief can be a smart move but it isn't always worth paying for. Here's when it could pay off.

item.title: Tennis icon Novak Djokovic talks aging in the sport and what he still wants to accomplish
item.description: Tennis legend Novak Djokovic talks with "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King about continuing to play at an elite level at the age of 39, Serena Williams' return to tennis and what he still hopes to accomplish in his career.

item.title: India activist urged to end hunger strike as he risks organ failure
item.description: Well-known activist Sonam Wangchuk hasn't eaten in nearly 3 weeks, endangering his health as he demands changes to India's exam system.

item.title: Maine Democratic Senate candidates criticize ICE during debate
item.description: Maine Democrats will choose a new Senate nominee after Graham Platner's exit from the race. This comes after an ICE officer in Maine fatally shot a man, renewing heated discussions over the role of the agency. CBS News' Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: "Master yachtsman" fugitive captured on boat after 21 years on the run
item.description: Ronald L. Fischer, who was featured on "America's Most Wanted," fled Rhode Island during his criminal trial for first-degree sexual assault in 2005.

item.title: Fact-checking Trump's speech on election security
item.description: President Trump delivered a speech on election security Thursday night at the White House. Here are the facts behind some of his claims.

item.title: Trump revisits disputed claims about elections but offers no new proof of fraud
item.description: In a primetime address, President Trump alleged the U.S. election system falls "catastrophically short," revisiting a topic that has drawn his attention for years — and making claims that election experts have heavily disputed.

item.title: Teleprompter operator made over $100K betting on Trump speeches
item.description: After detecting suspicious activity on the teleprompter operator's account, Kalshi investigated and then referred the case to federal regulators.

item.title: Nearly 736,000 Pillsbury rolls recalled over possible glass contamination
item.description: The recall includes cases of Pillsbury "Hard Roll Dough" and "Kaiser Roll Dough" bread rolls, which are marketed to businesses.

item.title: Cyclospora outbreak leaves consumers guessing which foods are safe
item.description: Officials are still searching for the source of the outbreak, prompting consumers to seek advice on social media about which foods to avoid.

item.title: Who will win the World Cup: Spain or Argentina?
item.description: Roger Bennett, founder and CEO of Men in Blazers Media Network, breaks down Argentina's thrilling win against England and what to expect from the World Cup final on Sunday when the defending champions take on Spain.

item.title: "Jurassic Park" star Sam Neill's cause of death revealed
item.description: The actor's agent said he was providing more information following news reports "which contain inaccuracies and outright falsehoods."
